novelty_basis: explicit differentiation against recent Computers and Geotechnics suffusion literature across pore-scale DEM/PFV/CFD/DFM, probabilistic fines-loss and constitutive suffusion papers; the claim is selective dam-scale FEM-Dijkstra-DEM activation, not a superior general suffusion model
validation_scope: Bounded external laboratory-state screening comparison against Lee et al. (2021), plus an independent Ke and Takahashi (2012) second-family residual endpoint check for particle-loss magnitude, plus numerical verification, executable FEM-Dijkstra-DEM implementation, sensitivity/mesh checks and reproducibility. This is not operational field validation or independent validation of the complete dam model.
sample_size_note: External validation remains small; conclusions remain bounded to screening logic.

ke_takahashi_2012_second_family_residual.claim_boundary: This is a small residual endpoint validation on Ke and Takahashi (2012) Specimen A-60 Table 6. It is independent of Lee et al. (2021), but it is not field validation, not a full trajectory validation, and not Sterpi/Chang/Zhu residual closure.
